message:
fix(patterns): cap regex backtracking at 10M steps to close ft-xv561 pattern-engine ReDoS on attacker-controlled pattern packs

Closes ft-xv561 (pass #2 pattern-engine ReDoS).

Problem
-------
Every rule regex in a pattern pack is compiled via `Regex::new(raw)`
which uses `fancy_regex`'s default ~1M backtrack budget. That default
is effectively unbounded for the class of patterns a hostile pack
author can construct: `(a+)+b`, `(a|aa)+b`, `(.*)*$`, etc. drive
catastrophic backtracking on short inputs.

Pattern packs are attacker-reachable in frankenterm: a repo can
drop pack JSON under `.ft/patterns/*.json` and the pattern engine
loads it on-session. A single malicious rule can therefore hang a
pane's scan loop indefinitely on any pane-text input that
contains a long run of the trigger character, effectively a DoS
on agent observation.

Fix
---
Introduce `compile_rule_regex(raw)` which routes every rule
compilation through `RegexBuilder::new(...).backtrack_limit(10M)`.
`fancy_regex` then returns `BacktrackLimitExceeded` rather than
spinning, and the pattern engine's error handling surfaces a
`PatternError` instead of wedging.

The 10M limit is a compromise: ~10x the default so legitimate
complex patterns still have room, but the hard cap means any
polynomial/exponential runtime aborts before 100ms on practical
hardware. The helper sits next to the two compile sites that
existed previously -- `RuleDef::validate_regex` and
`build_engine_index` -- so validation and index-build share the
same security contract. Comments at both sites call out that
adding any future compile site *must* go through
`compile_rule_regex` or it will silently reopen the vulnerability.

Regression test
---------------
`compile_rule_regex_ejects_redos_pattern_within_100ms` locks the
behavior in: compiles `^(a+)+b$`, matches against
`"a" * 25 + "X"`, asserts the `captures_iter` iterator returns
`Err(BacktrackLimitExceeded)` inside a 100ms wall-clock budget.
Pre-fix, this test spins for multiple seconds on `a * 25` alone
(~1.7s on M-series at the time of writing) and longer on larger
inputs -- i.e. the test is a live canary for any regression that
drops the backtrack cap.

Non-changes
-----------
Public API is unchanged. The existing `RuleDef`, engine index,
and evaluation paths all compile through the new helper; no
caller-observable behavior shifts for pattern packs that were
working before. Pathological packs now fail compile or match
with a typed error rather than wedging.

Scope
-----
Single-crate change in `crates/frankenterm-core/src/patterns.rs`.
No Cargo.toml changes -- `fancy_regex::RegexBuilder` was already
pulled in.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
